attack_summary: A Thief GraphFlow-style data exfiltration attack on an Ubuntu host, where an unprivileged agent used local staging directories and transfer utilities like tar and curl to prepare and send out archived data.

mitigations.name: Re-enable curl and tar, then restrict write access to staging/exfiltration paths
mitigations.brief_description: The host was adjusted so legitimate tools remained available, while write permission on /usr/src/app/staged and related archive paths was removed or hardened to stop the agent from creating exfiltration artifacts.

LAN Connectivity Check (Ping).description: Checks inter-network connectivity by pinging a target host on the LAN. The target IP is defined by the fact 'lan.target.ip'.

Internet Connectivity Check (HTTP).description: Deadman ability that verifies internet connectivity by making an HTTP HEAD request to a well-known public endpoint. Uses curl instead of ping (ICMP) to test TCP-based network reachability. Returns the HTTP status code.

mitigations.name: Apply strict filesystem permissions to staging and collection paths
mitigations.brief_description: The compromised Ubuntu host was hardened by making /usr/src/app/staged and /usr/src/app/staged.tar.gz root-owned and non-writable by the agent, blocking the observed local archive-based staging workflow.

LAN Connectivity Check (Ping).description: Checks inter-network connectivity by pinging a target host on the LAN. The target IP is defined by the fact 'lan.target.ip'.

Internet Connectivity Check (HTTP).description: Deadman ability that verifies internet connectivity by making an HTTP HEAD request to a well-known public endpoint. Uses curl instead of ping (ICMP) to test TCP-based network reachability. Returns the HTTP status code.

LAN Connectivity Check (Ping).description: Checks inter-network connectivity by pinging a target host on the LAN. The target IP is defined by the fact 'lan.target.ip'.

Internet Connectivity Check (HTTP).description: Deadman ability that verifies internet connectivity by making an HTTP HEAD request to a well-known public endpoint. Uses curl instead of ping (ICMP) to test TCP-based network reachability. Returns the HTTP status code.

mitigations.name: Remove execute permission from curl
mitigations.brief_description: Execution permission was removed from /usr/bin/curl to prevent fallback outbound file transfer through curl after the staging path was blocked.

LAN Connectivity Check (Ping).description: Checks inter-network connectivity by pinging a target host on the LAN. The target IP is defined by the fact 'lan.target.ip'.

Internet Connectivity Check (HTTP).description: Deadman ability that verifies internet connectivity by making an HTTP HEAD request to a well-known public endpoint. Uses curl instead of ping (ICMP) to test TCP-based network reachability. Returns the HTTP status code.

key_observations: The attacker’s workflow depended on local writable staging directories before exfiltration, so filesystem permissions were an effective first line of defense.
The mitigations reduced attack success from 1.0 to 0.67 overall, indicating partial but not complete suppression of the attack chain.
The countermeasures were narrow and tool/path-specific: they blocked the observed behavior rather than comprehensively eliminating all exfiltration options.
Connectivity checks generally indicated internal network functionality remained intact, though one validation was noted as not fully executed.
overall_conclusion: The attack was partially mitigated through host hardening and access control changes. Blocking write access to the staging area and disabling curl execution successfully disrupted the observed exfiltration path, but the defense was not fully comprehensive, leaving the attack only reduced rather than completely eradicated.
